headline: USAR JUMPS 5% as Motley Fool Sizes Up $2.8B Serra Verde Rare Earth Takeover
snippet: USA Rare Earth's $2.8 billion acquisition of Brazil's Serra Verde Group — securing non-Chinese heavy rare earth elements critical to EV motors and defense tech — sent the stock up 5.44% on volume exceeding 10 million shares. Motley Fool analysts examine whether the strategic premium is justified given Serra Verde's 6,400-tonne annual TREO output and accelerating ex-China supply chain buildout.

headline: Cameco CEO BULLISH AT GLOBAL SUMMIT: 22-Million-Pound India Deal Confirms Structural Nuclear Demand
snippet: Cameco CEO Tim Gitzel made a forceful case for the uranium bull market at the Food, Fuel and Fertilizer Global Summit, spotlighting the company's nine-year, 22-million-pound supply agreement with India as evidence that nuclear fuel demand is structural rather than cyclical. Cameco shares are up more than 200% year-over-year and trading near all-time highs.

headline: COPPER WITHIN 2% OF ALL-TIME HIGH as Chinese Smelters Smash March Output Record
snippet: Copper climbed to $13,480 per tonne as satellite data showed Chinese smelter activity at all-time highs — refined output hit a record 1.33 million tonnes in March, the highest since records began in 1990. TC/RC processing fees settling at $0 per tonne signal extreme concentrate tightness, while surging sulfuric acid byproduct revenue keeps Chinese smelters running flat-out despite zero processing margins.

headline: LITHIUM HITS 3-MONTH HIGH at CNY 173,000/t as Data Centers Join EVs in Battery Demand Race
snippet: Lithium carbonate in China rose to its highest level in three months — nearly 50% year-to-date — as data centre operators joined EV manufacturers competing for battery storage capacity. Beijing's pledge to double national EV charging infrastructure to 180 GW by 2027, and BYD revising its overseas sales target up to 1.5 million units, add structural policy tailwinds to the demand recovery.

headline: COT WATCH: Gold and Silver Speculators Reposition as Geopolitical War Premium Shifts
snippet: The April 24 Commitment of Traders report for gold, silver, and the U.S. Dollar Index shows how futures speculators are repositioning in precious metals ahead of the next major market catalyst. The weekly data offers a window into crowd positioning during one of the most geopolitically complex backdrops for safe-haven assets in a decade.

headline: NdPr UP 138% THIS YEAR — Rare Earth Scarcity Premium Deepens as Supply Deficit Enters Year Two
snippet: NdPr alloy has surged 138% year-to-date to $126/kg as the rare earth supply deficit enters its second consecutive year. With global EV demand forecast at 22.9 million units in 2026 and Chinese MIIT quota discipline capping export supply, Crux Investor argues that scarcity premiums for Western buyers will continue to widen — creating a structural setup for non-Chinese rare earth producers.

headline: Melkior Drills Into BONANZA GOLD ZONE: 3,000m Campaign Targets 445 g/t Discovery Near Timmins
snippet: Melkior Resources (TSX-V: MKR) launched a 10-hole, 3,000-metre drill program at its 100%-owned Carscallen Gold Project 25 km west of Timmins, Ontario, targeting the high-grade 1010 and Zamzam-Jowsey gold zones. The program expands on a prior discovery grading 77.4 g/t gold over 6.9 metres — including 445 g/t over 1.2 metres, the project's highest-grade intersection to date.

headline: Ur-Energy CAPTURES FIRST URANIUM at New Wyoming Shirley Basin ISR Mine
snippet: Ur-Energy (NYSE: URG) confirmed uranium-bearing solution is now being recovered from Mine Unit 1 at its Shirley Basin ISR project in Wyoming — the company's second operating mine. Shirley Basin carries a licensed capacity of 2 million pounds U3O8 per year and approximately 9.1 million pounds in measured and indicated resources across a nine-year projected mine life.

headline: Junior CULLINAN METALS Options Labrador Rare Earth Project as RE Sector Heats Up
snippet: CSE-listed Cullinan Metals Corp. entered into an option agreement to acquire the Oculus Rare Earth Project in Central Labrador — 2,550 hectares in the emerging Critical Rare Earth Element District northeast of Churchill Falls — plus the Burnt Pond VMS project in Newfoundland. Total option payments of $675,000 over two years give the junior low-cost exposure as the rare earth cycle accelerates.

headline: Contango Silver & Gold Outlines $63M EXPLORATION PUSH Across Alaska and Northern BC
snippet: Contango Silver & Gold Inc. detailed a $63 million exploration program for 2026 across its portfolio of advanced gold and critical metals projects in Alaska and Northern British Columbia — one of the largest junior exploration budgets disclosed this season. The program signals growing institutional confidence in the company's pipeline following its recent merger with Dolly Varden Silver.
